title: centaurea atlantica subsp. calvescens
intro: Centaurea atlantica subsp. calvescens is a subspecies of the Atlas cornflower plant, a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Centaurea atlantica subsp. calvescens include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
